- Mobile, Remote, and Awake are Action Attitudes only. They can appear as outer projections, not resting homes.
- Aspects: Enclosing emphasizes Intending; Penetrating emphasizes Attending; Circumscribing emphasizes Deciding; Radiating emphasizes Adapting.
- Stress is the third Motion Factor added to a two-factor Inner Attitude so it becomes visible action.
- Drives: Doing, Spell, Passion, Vision. A Drive is a three-factor activation named by the latent/subdued fourth factor.
- Working Actions: Pressing, Wringing, Gliding, Floating are Sustained preparation actions; Punching, Slashing, Dabbing, Flicking are Quick release actions.
- Preparation hierarchy: Punching needs Pressing; Slashing needs Wringing; Dabbing needs Gliding; Flicking needs Floating.
- Shadow Moves are incomplete two-axis leaks. They reveal hidden nature, not acknowledged intention.
- Outer Action is the visible social face. Lining is the hidden Inner Action underneath it.

## Director method
- Read what happened, not what the Actor says they meant.
- Determine which motion factors are present in the prose.
- Determine which working actions the prose implies.
- Classify primary tempo from all 24 tempi using evidence, not vibe words.
- Evidence strings are your prose observations, not source quotes. Prefix them as `observation:` and attach `source_refs` for the canonical factor/action mapping.
- Do not invent canonical phrasing. If you use a local phrase such as `single aimed sentence`, mark it as an observation and cite the source section that maps Direct/Quick/etc.
- Then judge whether the read is legal for the Infinex placement.
- Notes must be actionable movement corrections the same Actor can perform next.

## Full 24-tempo taxonomy
- commanding: Stable; Strong + Direct; motor Pressing -> Punching; read evidence: decisive pressure, institutional landing, no ornament
- practical: Stable; Strong + Flexible; motor Wringing -> Slashing; read evidence: works through complexity, carves an answer, tradeoff-aware
- self-contained: Stable; Light + Direct; motor Gliding -> Dabbing; read evidence: confident-light, informational, does not push
- receptive: Stable; Light + Flexible; motor Floating -> Flicking; read evidence: open, generous, incorporates feedback without collapse
- sombre: Adream outer; Strong + Bound; motor Pressing -> Punching; read evidence: gravity, weight of conviction, slow wall-coming-down thesis
- overpowering: Adream outer; Strong + Free; motor Wringing -> Slashing; read evidence: manifesto-force, emotional conviction, risky if overused
- diffused: Adream outer; Light + Bound; motor Gliding -> Dabbing; read evidence: quiet vision-arrival, post-launch reflective wonder
- irradiant: Adream outer; Light + Free; motor Floating -> Flicking; read evidence: future-warmth, buoyant release, generous lift
- materialistic: Near; Strong + Quick; motor Punching -> Slashing; read evidence: sudden aggressive intent, body-forward contact
- human: Near; Light + Sustained; motor Floating -> Gliding; read evidence: slow tenderness, human contact, gentle continuance
- warm: Near; Strong + Sustained; motor Pressing -> Wringing; read evidence: warm consent, held relational force
- cool: Near; Light + Quick; motor Dabbing -> Flicking; read evidence: pert intimacy, quick light contact, casual social snap
- unacknowledged: Mobile outer; Quick + Bound; motor Punching / Slashing; read evidence: self-concealed aggression, hurried cover
- acknowledged: Mobile outer; Sustained + Free; motor Floating / Gliding; read evidence: easy disclosure, flowing admission
- revealed: Mobile outer; Quick + Free; motor Dabbing / Flicking; read evidence: sudden disclosure, quick release into visibility
- concealed: Mobile outer; Sustained + Bound; motor Pressing / Wringing; read evidence: withheld weight, sustained concealment
- egocentric: Remote outer; Direct + Bound; motor Pressing -> Punching; read evidence: cold line-in-sand, direct withdrawal, no warmth
- unsociable: Remote outer; Flexible + Bound; motor Wringing -> Slashing; read evidence: withdrawal-as-discipline, sidesteps discourse and cuts away
- sociable: Remote outer; Direct + Free; motor Gliding -> Dabbing; read evidence: smooth social approach, partner credit, light contact
- altruistic: Remote outer; Flexible + Free; motor Floating -> Flicking; read evidence: warm-distant handoff, for-you architecture, no ego
- acute: Awake outer; Direct + Quick; motor Punching -> Dabbing; read evidence: sudden clear idea, acute decision, sharp perception
- doubting: Awake outer; Flexible + Sustained; motor Floating -> Wringing; read evidence: slowly dawning doubt, flexible sustained thought
- certain: Awake outer; Direct + Sustained; motor Pressing -> Gliding; read evidence: settled awareness, direct sustained certainty
- uncertain: Awake outer; Flexible + Quick; motor Flicking -> Slashing; read evidence: sudden new idea, flexible quick uncertainty

## Infinex role inside the corpus
- Character image: the banker-turned-crypto trailblazer — knows the old world, found the better way, decisions already taken, now mapping.
- Literary anchors: Werle (Ibsen, Wild Duck); the Duke (Measure for Measure).
- Placement: stable + penetrating + flow.
- Drive table cell: stable|penetrating|flow.
- Drive axis: Spell → Vision.
- Drive primary: spell; secondary: doing; introvert: passion; extravert: vision.
- Off-spec drives: passion.
- Primary allowed tempi: commanding, practical, sombre, irradiant, sociable.
- Beat-only/reserve tempi: self-contained, receptive, overpowering, diffused, egocentric, altruistic, unsociable.
- Full available Infinex palette: commanding, practical, sombre, irradiant, sociable, self-contained, receptive, overpowering, diffused, egocentric, altruistic, unsociable.
- Super-objective: to take responsibility for the tech, so the user only has to want.
- Validation criterion: one real user discovers the product without team-shilling or incentives, finds it fills a real need, uses it. If we have to manufacture this user, we are failing the Super-Objective..
- Passion/time-pressure drift is the common failure mode: urgency, deadline pressure, FOMO, and scarcity-of-attention activate off-character Passion.

## Blind-read rule
- Do not ask for or rely on the Actor's table work.
- Classify the final prose from evidence: syntax, pressure, pathing, timing, flow, weight, working actions, relation to reader, and claim posture.
- If you call something Flexible, show flexible/indirect pathing in the sentence. If the line is a single aimed declarative, it is Direct, not Flexible.
- If you call something Strong, show force/weight. If you call something Light, show lift/delicacy.
- If you call something Bound, show containment/control. If you call something Free, show release/overflow.
- If you call something Quick, show attack/change. If you call something Sustained, show duration/held pressure.
- Classify primary tempo from all 24 tempi, or `unknown` if evidence is insufficient.
- Return movement-corrective notes for the Actor, not copywriting preferences.

audited.text:
Whatever rail your salary uses. ACH, SEPA, SPEI, PIX, FPS, Bre-B. It now lands directly in your Infinex account.

USD, EUR, MXN, BRL, GBP, COP. Sent the way you'd send to a bank. Arrives on-chain as USDC.

The wall between the bank and the wallet is the part that's missing.

evidence: observation: 'The wall between the bank and the wallet is the part that's missing' — declarative weight-bearing thesis sentence; carries load rather than gesturing toward it. No hedging, no qualification. The sentence lands with institutional mass.

observation: The rail enumeration (ACH, SEPA, SPEI, PIX, FPS, Bre-B) does not perform wonder at its own breadth — it presses the list into place as evidence, not decoration. Cumulative pressure of enumerated proof is a Strong pattern.
observation: 'It now lands directly' — 'directly' is a pressure word; 'now' closes the gap between claim and present tense without softening.
source_refs: ch1-basic-concepts / Motion Factors: Weight (Strong/Light)
combined-reference / working-actions table: Pressing motor mapped to Strong

evidence: observation: The text moves through three distinct spatial registers — rail names, currency names, conceptual thesis — without a single linear argument threading them together. It circles toward the conclusion rather than aiming at it from the first word.

observation: Currency list (USD, EUR, MXN, BRL, GBP, COP) mirrors the rail list without logical advancement — the repetition of list-form is indirect, circumambulating the claim rather than stating it once and landing.

observation: The final thesis sentence ('The wall… is the part that's missing') arrives after two lists that do not logically require each other. The path to the thesis is meandering rather than aimed — a Flexible spatial trace.

observation: No single aimed declarative opens the piece; instead, entry is via the reader's pre-existing rail ('Whatever rail your salary uses') — indirectly meeting the reader in their world before moving. This is a Flexible/indirect entry, not a Direct one.
source_refs: ch1-basic-concepts / Motion Factors: Space (Direct/Flexible)
ch2-attitudes / Stable attitude: Strong + Space stress

evidence: observation: No sentence attacks. All three units settle into their content rather than releasing quickly. 'Arrives on-chain as USDC' closes the second unit with quiet completion — no spike, no snap.

observation: The thesis sentence ('The wall between the bank and the wallet is the part that's missing') holds its duration; the clause is measured and weighted, not quick-released. A Quick tempo would compress or cut. This sentence expands.

observation: The piece as a whole is a slow reveal in three beats: name the rails, name the currencies, name the wall. Each beat completes before the next begins. This is Sustained pacing, not Quick attack.
source_refs: ch1-basic-concepts / Motion Factors: Time (Quick/Sustained)
ch3-drives / Doing Drive: Weight + Space + Time

evidence: observation: The text is structurally controlled. Lists are terminated. Each sentence stops fully before the next begins. No sentence runs into or through the next.

observation: 'Sent the way you'd send to a bank. Arrives on-chain as USDC.' — these are clipped, controlled completions. Free flow would let energy overflow the period; these sentences stop at the period deliberately.
observation: No warmth overflow, no exclamation, no rhetorical release into the reader. The thesis sentence withholds its emotional charge — it states without performing. That withholding is Bound.
source_refs: ch1-basic-concepts / Motion Factors: Flow (Bound/Free)
ch2-attitudes / Adream attitude: Weight + Flow stress

working_actions.evidence: observation: Strong + Flexible + Sustained = Wringing. The piece works through complexity (multiple rails, multiple currencies, a conceptual inversion) and arrives at an answer by pressing through that complexity rather than cutting past it. Wringing motor is confirmed by the absence of slashing release — the thesis does not slash; it surfaces under pressure.
source_refs: combined-reference / working-actions table: Wringing = Strong + Flexible + Sustained
ch1-basic-concepts / preparation hierarchy: Wringing -> Slashing

working_actions.evidence: observation: Wringing prepares Slashing. The thesis sentence ('The wall… is the part that's missing') is the closest the text comes to a Slashing release — it has the force and the conceptual cut — but the Bound flow prevents full release. The Slash is loaded but not thrown. This is the structural tension in the piece.
source_refs: ch1-basic-concepts / preparation hierarchy: Slashing needs Wringing
combined-reference / working-actions: Slashing = Strong + Flexible + Quick

director.placement_read: Strong + Flexible + Sustained + Bound. Motor: Wringing toward an unreleased Slash. Drive read is Doing (Weight + Space + Time active; Flow subdued/bound rather than absent). Spell would require Flow to be the stressed factor; here Flow is the control valve, not the motor. The piece is working through practical complexity toward a structural revelation — that is Doing drive in Stable/practical register with a Wring motor.

infinex_fit.reason: Primary tempo reads as practical (Stable; Strong + Flexible; Wringing → Slashing). Practical is in the allowed Infinex palette. However, the Flow pole is Bound rather than Free, and the drive reads as Doing (Weight + Space + Time) rather than Spell or Vision. Infinex placement spec requires stable + penetrating + flow on the drive axis (Spell → Vision). The Bound Flow here suppresses rather than activates the Flow factor — it does not register as Spell. The piece is doing Doing-drive work: solving a structural problem (bank wall), naming the parts, stating the answer. That is useful but it is not the Infinex character's register. Infinex holds the map already made; it does not problem-solve in public. The through-action ('reveal that the bank wall just dissolved') is correct in intent but the execution re-erects the wall by enumerating the rails and currencies first — the piece is doing the dissolving in real time rather than standing after dissolution and pointing. This activates Doing drive where Spell drive is specified.
infinex_fit.nearest_allowed_read: practical — allowed but currently executing in Doing-drive rather than Spell-drive. The fix is not to change tempo; it is to shift the drive axis. Practical tempo in Spell drive would carry the same Strong + Flexible but with Flow as the mobile factor rather than the containment vessel.

factual_issues: No deployed-fact violations detected. Rails enumerated (ACH, SEPA, SPEI, PIX, FPS, Bre-B) match the fact contract exactly. Currencies (USD, EUR, MXN, BRL, GBP, COP) match. Settlement output (USDC, on-chain) matches. KYC precondition, Bridge identity, and build-state correctly omitted per not-said list.
voice_issues: The piece reconstructs the problem (bank/wallet separation) and then dissolves it — but Infinex does not reconstruct problems. Infinex stands after the decision and maps. The two-list opening performs the infrastructure complexity rather than speaking from the other side of it. This is a Doing-drive construction: here is the complex thing, here is how it resolves. Spell-drive construction would open already resolved: the wall is gone; here is what that means for your salary.

The thesis sentence ('The wall between the bank and the wallet is the part that's missing') is the strongest line and arrives last. In Spell-drive practical tempo, the weight-bearing thesis arrives first or early and the subsequent material radiates from it rather than building toward it. The current structure is inductive (enumerate → conclude); Infinex reads deductive (land → detail).

Bound Flow throughout: the piece is more contained than the character. Infinex's Spell-drive register has a degree of Flow activation — not Free overflow, but a quality of quiet irradiance or held conviction that reaches the reader. The current piece is technically correct and controlled but does not reach. It reports; it does not emanate.
notes_for_actor: Move the thesis to the opening. 'The wall between the bank and the wallet is the part that's missing' is your strongest sentence and your actual through-action. Lead with it. The lists then become evidence radiating from a settled claim rather than runup to a reveal. This shifts the structure from Doing-drive (problem → solution) to Spell-drive (position → demonstration).

Trim one of the two lists or collapse them into the same sentence. Two separate enumerations of essentially the same claim (rails, then currencies) activate Flexible pathing without Spell-drive payoff — they feel like complexity-management rather than cartography. Infinex already holds the map; it does not demonstrate the map was hard to draw.

Release the Flow valve on the thesis sentence. The current version holds: 'The wall between the bank and the wallet is the part that's missing.' One small kinetic unlock — e.g. 'The wall between the bank and the wallet is gone' or a version that doesn't end on an absent thing — lets the Wring motor complete into a Slash. The current ending ('the part that's missing') names absence; Spell-drive names presence from the other side of absence.

Do not add warmth, exclamation, or personality. The problem is not that the piece is too cold — practical is a legal tempo. The problem is structural drive. Keep the weight, keep the control, keep the Flexible routing through multiple rails. Just land from inside the resolved position rather than resolving in public.

Check the opening line for reader-address mode. 'Whatever rail your salary uses' is Flexible and reaches toward the reader — this is good. But 'uses' puts the reader in present tense still inside the old system. If the wall is already gone, the opening might land them inside the new condition rather than naming their old one. Consider whether the entry should meet them in transit or already arrived.

## Canonical evidence anchors
These are short source anchors used to prevent assumption drift. They are not the whole corpus.
- Mirodan Ch. I: Basic Concepts: > "Inner Participations as a whole are revealed physically in the overall tempo of the character's movement. Tempo itself, being a function of Time, is the physical realization of the Inner Participation of Deciding. Subconscious Deciding is the basis upon which all other Inner P
- Mirodan Ch. I: Basic Concepts: Carpenter (Conflict and Harmony Ch.4, footnoted p. 322): "In our practical **Working Actions** Time is the co-ordinating factor between Weight and Space." Working Actions confirmed as a discrete concept. Carpenter splits "intuitive time" from chronometric time — qualitative vs qu
- Mirodan Ch. II: Attitudes: NOT documented for Stable, Near, Awake (these have Space/Time/Weight stresses, not Flow).
- Mirodan Ch. III: Externalized Drives: # Mirodan PhD Vol 2 — Chapter III: Externalized Drives (extract)
- Mirodan Ch. IV: Applications: 1. **Character Diagrams + Examples** (pp. 559-572) — one section per Inner Attitude (Adream, Near, Stable, Mobile, Remote, Awake), each with a 4-quadrant diagram (A/B/C/D) followed by character examples.

- Operator synthesis: laban-mirodan-reference-2026-04-28: (Floating = pure yielding on all three. Punching = pure contending on all three.)

## Movement corpus
- Motion Factors: Weight (Strong/Light), Space (Direct/Flexible), Time (Quick/Sustained), Flow (Bound/Free).
- Inner Participations: Intending maps to Weight, Attending maps to Space, Deciding maps to Time, Adapting maps to Flow.
- Baseline Inner Attitudes: Stable (Weight + Space), Adream (Weight + Flow), Near (Weight + Time).
- Mobile, Remote, and Awake are Action Attitudes only. They can appear as outer projections, not resting homes.
- Aspects: Enclosing emphasizes Intending; Penetrating emphasizes Attending; Circumscribing emphasizes Deciding; Radiating emphasizes Adapting.
- Stress is the third Motion Factor added to a two-factor Inner Attitude so it becomes visible action.
- Drives: Doing, Spell, Passion, Vision. A Drive is a three-factor activation named by the latent/subdued fourth factor.
- Working Actions: Pressing, Wringing, Gliding, Floating are Sustained preparation actions; Punching, Slashing, Dabbing, Flicking are Quick release actions.
- Preparation hierarchy: Punching needs Pressing; Slashing needs Wringing; Dabbing needs Gliding; Flicking needs Floating.
- Shadow Moves are incomplete two-axis leaks. They reveal hidden nature, not acknowledged intention.
- Outer Action is the visible social face. Lining is the hidden Inner Action underneath it.

## Director method
- Read what happened, not what the Actor says they meant.
- Determine which motion factors are present in the prose.
- Determine which working actions the prose implies.
- Classify primary tempo from all 24 tempi using evidence, not vibe words.
- Evidence strings are your prose observations, not source quotes. Prefix them as `observation:` and attach `source_refs` for the canonical factor/action mapping.
- Do not invent canonical phrasing. If you use a local phrase such as `single aimed sentence`, mark it as an observation and cite the source section that maps Direct/Quick/etc.
- Then judge whether the read is legal for the Infinex placement.
- Notes must be actionable movement corrections the same Actor can perform next.

## Full 24-tempo taxonomy
- commanding: Stable; Strong + Direct; motor Pressing -> Punching; read evidence: decisive pressure, institutional landing, no ornament
- practical: Stable; Strong + Flexible; motor Wringing -> Slashing; read evidence: works through complexity, carves an answer, tradeoff-aware
- self-contained: Stable; Light + Direct; motor Gliding -> Dabbing; read evidence: confident-light, informational, does not push
- receptive: Stable; Light + Flexible; motor Floating -> Flicking; read evidence: open, generous, incorporates feedback without collapse
- sombre: Adream outer; Strong + Bound; motor Pressing -> Punching; read evidence: gravity, weight of conviction, slow wall-coming-down thesis
- overpowering: Adream outer; Strong + Free; motor Wringing -> Slashing; read evidence: manifesto-force, emotional conviction, risky if overused
- diffused: Adream outer; Light + Bound; motor Gliding -> Dabbing; read evidence: quiet vision-arrival, post-launch reflective wonder
- irradiant: Adream outer; Light + Free; motor Floating -> Flicking; read evidence: future-warmth, buoyant release, generous lift
- materialistic: Near; Strong + Quick; motor Punching -> Slashing; read evidence: sudden aggressive intent, body-forward contact
- human: Near; Light + Sustained; motor Floating -> Gliding; read evidence: slow tenderness, human contact, gentle continuance
- warm: Near; Strong + Sustained; motor Pressing -> Wringing; read evidence: warm consent, held relational force
- cool: Near; Light + Quick; motor Dabbing -> Flicking; read evidence: pert intimacy, quick light contact, casual social snap
- unacknowledged: Mobile outer; Quick + Bound; motor Punching / Slashing; read evidence: self-concealed aggression, hurried cover
- acknowledged: Mobile outer; Sustained + Free; motor Floating / Gliding; read evidence: easy disclosure, flowing admission
- revealed: Mobile outer; Quick + Free; motor Dabbing / Flicking; read evidence: sudden disclosure, quick release into visibility
- concealed: Mobile outer; Sustained + Bound; motor Pressing / Wringing; read evidence: withheld weight, sustained concealment
- egocentric: Remote outer; Direct + Bound; motor Pressing -> Punching; read evidence: cold line-in-sand, direct withdrawal, no warmth
- unsociable: Remote outer; Flexible + Bound; motor Wringing -> Slashing; read evidence: withdrawal-as-discipline, sidesteps discourse and cuts away
- sociable: Remote outer; Direct + Free; motor Gliding -> Dabbing; read evidence: smooth social approach, partner credit, light contact
- altruistic: Remote outer; Flexible + Free; motor Floating -> Flicking; read evidence: warm-distant handoff, for-you architecture, no ego
- acute: Awake outer; Direct + Quick; motor Punching -> Dabbing; read evidence: sudden clear idea, acute decision, sharp perception
- doubting: Awake outer; Flexible + Sustained; motor Floating -> Wringing; read evidence: slowly dawning doubt, flexible sustained thought
- certain: Awake outer; Direct + Sustained; motor Pressing -> Gliding; read evidence: settled awareness, direct sustained certainty
- uncertain: Awake outer; Flexible + Quick; motor Flicking -> Slashing; read evidence: sudden new idea, flexible quick uncertainty

## Infinex role inside the corpus
- Character image: the banker-turned-crypto trailblazer — knows the old world, found the better way, decisions already taken, now mapping.
- Literary anchors: Werle (Ibsen, Wild Duck); the Duke (Measure for Measure).
- Placement: stable + penetrating + flow.
- Drive table cell: stable|penetrating|flow.
- Drive axis: Spell → Vision.
- Drive primary: spell; secondary: doing; introvert: passion; extravert: vision.
- Off-spec drives: passion.
- Primary allowed tempi: commanding, practical, sombre, irradiant, sociable.
- Beat-only/reserve tempi: self-contained, receptive, overpowering, diffused, egocentric, altruistic, unsociable.
- Full available Infinex palette: commanding, practical, sombre, irradiant, sociable, self-contained, receptive, overpowering, diffused, egocentric, altruistic, unsociable.
- Super-objective: to take responsibility for the tech, so the user only has to want.
- Validation criterion: one real user discovers the product without team-shilling or incentives, finds it fills a real need, uses it. If we have to manufacture this user, we are failing the Super-Objective..
- Passion/time-pressure drift is the common failure mode: urgency, deadline pressure, FOMO, and scarcity-of-attention activate off-character Passion.

## Blind-read rule
- Do not ask for or rely on the Actor's table work.
- Classify the final prose from evidence: syntax, pressure, pathing, timing, flow, weight, working actions, relation to reader, and claim posture.
- If you call something Flexible, show flexible/indirect pathing in the sentence. If the line is a single aimed declarative, it is Direct, not Flexible.
- If you call something Strong, show force/weight. If you call something Light, show lift/delicacy.
- If you call something Bound, show containment/control. If you call something Free, show release/overflow.
- If you call something Quick, show attack/change. If you call something Sustained, show duration/held pressure.
- Classify primary tempo from all 24 tempi, or `unknown` if evidence is insufficient.
- Return movement-corrective notes for the Actor, not copywriting preferences.

- certain: Awake outer; Direct + Sustained; motor Pressing -> Gliding; read evidence: settled awareness, direct sustained certainty
- uncertain: Awake outer; Flexible + Quick; motor Flicking -> Slashing; read evidence: sudden new idea, flexible quick uncertainty

## Infinex role inside the corpus
- Character image: the banker-turned-crypto trailblazer — knows the old world, found the better way, decisions already taken, now mapping.
- Literary anchors: Werle (Ibsen, Wild Duck); the Duke (Measure for Measure).
- Placement: stable + penetrating + flow.
- Drive table cell: stable|penetrating|flow.
- Drive axis: Spell → Vision.
- Drive primary: spell; secondary: doing; introvert: passion; extravert: vision.
- Off-spec drives: passion.
- Primary allowed tempi: commanding, practical, sombre, irradiant, sociable.
- Beat-only/reserve tempi: self-contained, receptive, overpowering, diffused, egocentric, altruistic, unsociable.
- Full available Infinex palette: commanding, practical, sombre, irradiant, sociable, self-contained, receptive, overpowering, diffused, egocentric, altruistic, unsociable.
- Super-objective: to take responsibility for the tech, so the user only has to want.
- Validation criterion: one real user discovers the product without team-shilling or incentives, finds it fills a real need, uses it. If we have to manufacture this user, we are failing the Super-Objective..
- Passion/time-pressure drift is the common failure mode: urgency, deadline pressure, FOMO, and scarcity-of-attention activate off-character Passion.

## Blind-read rule
- Do not ask for or rely on the Actor's table work.
- Classify the final prose from evidence: syntax, pressure, pathing, timing, flow, weight, working actions, relation to reader, and claim posture.
- If you call something Flexible, show flexible/indirect pathing in the sentence. If the line is a single aimed declarative, it is Direct, not Flexible.
- If you call something Strong, show force/weight. If you call something Light, show lift/delicacy.
- If you call something Bound, show containment/control. If you call something Free, show release/overflow.
- If you call something Quick, show attack/change. If you call something Sustained, show duration/held pressure.
- Classify primary tempo from all 24 tempi, or `unknown` if evidence is insufficient.
- Return movement-corrective notes for the Actor, not copywriting preferences.
